calendar.matters:
ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Mar-26-2009 Discharged

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-03-2008 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Aug-05-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Robert F. Cohen, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Nick Renzi, who was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendant Jagpal Batth, an individual.
Default entered against defendant on November 6, 2006.
Doe defendants dismissed on June 17, 2008.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Nick Renzi, and against the Defaulted Defendant Jagpal Batth, an individual, as follows:

Special Damages - $4,246.75.
General Damages - 20,000.00.
Punitife Damages - $50,000.00.
Costs - $463.10, for a total of $79,709.85.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating Sanctions And Entry
Off Calendar Nov-01-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Robert F. Cohen, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & moving party: Nick Renzi
No other appearance.

Ruling:
Hearing held. Plaintiff Nick Renzi's Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Entry Of Default is GRANTED. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-13-2007 Set For Trial On Mar-17-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Feb-27-08 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 604

Ntc And Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Aug-17-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Robert I. Lodmer, Esq., appeared for Defendant & opposing party: Nick Renzi
No other appearance.
Ruling:
Hearing held. Tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Nick Renzi's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories; Responses To Specially Prepared Interrogatories; Responses To And Production Pursuant To Demand For Production Of Documents; To Deem Admitted Requests For Admissions is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant Jagpal Batth to respond to Form Interrogatories, Specially Prepared Interrogatories, and to respond to and to produce documents pursuant To Demand For Production Of Documents within 15 days of notice of this order. The Requests For Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Defendant in the amount of $390, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
